title: SOP for Analysis of Benoxacor in Subcellular Fractions by GC-MS/ECD
author: Derek Simonsen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bb52iq8e

steps.title: Label disposable culture tubes and GC vials
steps.instruction: Label disposable culture tubes and GC vials. Tubes and vials that need to be labeled include quality assurance/quality control (QA/QC) samples (i.e., buffer, cofactor with no benoxacor, inactivated subcellular fractions, subcellular fractions without c-substrate, and DMSO spiked buffer with cofactor), experimental samples and the Reference Standard. Important: Use unique sample numbers that include your initials and identifiers for the experiment (e.g., notebook number) and the sample. The goal is to avoid identical sample numbers across experiments.

steps.title: At least one tube for method blanks (buffer only)
steps.instruction: At least one tube for method blanks (buffer only).

steps.title: At least one tube per sample...
steps.instruction: At least one tube per sample blank (matrix samples from species/sex analyzed).

steps.title: Three tubes for Ongoing Precision and...
steps.instruction: Three tubes for Ongoing Precision and Recovery (OPR) samples (matrix samples spiked with known amounts of the target analyte(s), i.e., benoxacor).

steps.title: Tubes for all samples (depends on...
steps.instruction: Tubes for all samples (depends on number of samples to be analyzed; it is recommended that 20% of the samples are QA/QC samples; increase the number of blank samples accordingly).

steps.title: One tube for the Reference Standard...
steps.instruction: One tube for the Reference Standard (will include known amounts of all analytes, including the surrogate recovery standards and internal standard) (Note: Solutions of all analytes are added to the tube with the Reference Standard at the same time when the samples are spiked)

steps.title: Immediately after incubation is completed place...
steps.instruction: Immediately after incubation is completed place samples in furnace for 10 minutes at 110 o C (Note: Leaving samples in furnace longer to 10 minutes will reduce the recovery of the analytes)

steps.title: Cool samples to room temperature before continuing extraction
steps.instruction: Cool samples to room temperature before continuing extraction.

steps.title: Spike samples with 25 μL of...
steps.instruction: Spike samples with 25 μL of 20 μg/mL trifluralin dissolved in ethyl acetate

steps.title: Add 2 mL pesticide grade ethyl...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL pesticide grade ethyl acetate to all samples (Note: Do not add extra ethyl acetate to reference)

steps.title: Put samples on tube rotator for...
steps.instruction: Put samples on tube rotator for 1 minute at 40 rpm to mix buffer and ethyl acetate. (Note:) Make sure caps are tight so they do not leak

steps.title: Transfer the samples to a centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer the samples to a centrifuge and centrifuge at 3,000 rpm (1811 x g) for 5 minutes to facilitate the phase separation

steps.title: Add 2 mL of 1% KCl...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of 1% KCl to the 4 mL of ethyl acetate to remove residual proteins.

steps.title: Put samples on tube rotator to...
steps.instruction: Put samples on tube rotator to mix the ethyl acetate and 1% KCl

steps.title: Transfer the samples to a centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer the samples to a centrifuge and centrifuge at 3,000 rpm (1811 x g) for 5 minutes to facilitate the phase separation

steps.title: Transfer 2 mL of ethyl acetate...
steps.instruction: Transfer 2 mL of ethyl acetate into a clean culture tube

steps.title: Repeat steps 3-5.1 (Note
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 3-5.1 (Note: Combine the 2 mL of ethyl acetate to the previous 2 mL ethyl acetate generated from the first extraction)

steps.title: Transfer the 4 mL ethyl acetate...
steps.instruction: Transfer the 4 mL ethyl acetate to a clean culture tube containing 0.25 mg of magnesium sulfate

steps.title: Briefly vortex each tube
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex each tube.

steps.title: Transfer the samples to a centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer the samples to a centrifuge and centrifuge at 3,000 rpm (1811 x g) for 5 minutes to facilitate precipitation of magnesium sulfate

steps.title: Carefully transfer the ethyl acetate into...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the ethyl acetate into a clean tube while attempting to not transfer any magnesium sulfate (Note: if small amounts of magnesium sulfate does get transferred it can be removed in subsequent steps)

steps.title: Spike each tube with 25 μL with 20 μg/mL acenaphthene
steps.instruction: Spike each tube with 25 μL with 20 μg/mL acenaphthene

steps.title: Evaporate each tube under nitrogen to approximately 500 μL
steps.instruction: Evaporate each tube under nitrogen to approximately 500 μL

steps.title: Transfer samples into a GC vial...
steps.instruction: Transfer samples into a GC vial and dilute to 1 mL

steps.title: Run samples on GC/MS followed by GC/ECD
steps.instruction: Run samples on GC/MS followed by GC/ECD.

steps.title: Example Quantification of Benoxacor
steps.instruction: Example Quantification of Benoxacor.xlsx

steps.title: GC/MS Parameters Samples were injected by...
steps.instruction: GC/MS Parameters Samples were injected by splitless injection at 280 o C. We used a Supelco SLB-5MS column (30 m length, 0.25 mm inner diameter, 0.25 µm film thickness) with a column helium flow rate of 1 mL/min. The oven parameters were programmed as follows: Initial temperature, 50 o C hold for 1 min, 15 o C/min to 240 o C, hold for 13 min, 15 o C/min to 300 o C, hold for 10 min. The temperatures of the MS source and the MS quad were 230 o C and 150 o C, respectively. A B C D 1 Compound Avg. Rention time Quant Qual 2 Acenaphthene 11.35 153 154 3 Trifluralin 12.74 264 306 4 Benoxacor 15.57 120 259 SIM Mode Monitored Ions

steps.title: GC-ECD Parameters A 63 Ni-µECD detector...
steps.instruction: GC-ECD Parameters A 63 Ni-µECD detector was used to monitor enantioselective metabolism. We used an Agilent CP-Chrialsil Dex CB (CB) column (25 m length, 0.25 mm inner diameter, 0.25 µm film thickness) to determine the enantiomeric fraction of benoxacor using the following temperature program: 50 o C, hold for 1 min, 10 o C/min to 145 o C, hold for 50 min, 15 o C/min to 200 o C, hold for 63 Ni-µECD detector 12 min. The injector temperature was set at 250 o C, and a constant helium flow rate of 2 mL/min was used.
